# anthropic_api_key (Resource)

Manages an existing Anthropic API key. The Admin API does NOT support creating keys — keys must be created in the Console first, then their `id` supplied here so this provider can manage `name` and `status` declaratively. Destroying this resource sets status to `archived`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# API keys cannot be created via the Admin API. Create the key in the Anthropic
# Console first, then reference its ID here to manage name/status declaratively.
resource "anthropic_api_key" "ci" {
  id     = "apikey_01Rj2N8SVvo6BePZj99NhmiT"
  name   = "ci-runner"
  status = "active"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) ID of an existing API key (e.g. `apikey_...`). Must already exist in your organization.

### Optional

- `name` (String) Human-readable key name.
- `status` (String) Key status: `active`, `inactive`, or `archived`. `expired` is read-only (set by the API).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `created_by_id` (String)
- `created_by_type` (String)
- `expires_at` (String)
- `partial_key_hint` (String)
- `workspace_id` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import anthropic_api_key.ci apikey_01Rj2N8SVvo6BePZj99NhmiT
```
